MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Saul M. Ferster, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF SAUL M. FERSTER (415-863-2678), for intervenor / moving party John E. Wai
Defendant / responding party Raymond G. Choy, (415-522-5201), appearing in pro per, and as husband of the Raymond G. Choy and Lorraine J. Choy Revocable Trust dated August 6, 2007

RULING:
INTERVENOR JOHN WAI'S CONTINUED MOTION TO ENFORCE JUDGMENT

Prior to the hearing the Court heard and ruled on intervenor's objections to Raymond Choy's declaration offered in opposition to intervenor's continued motion to enforce judgment. The evidentiary objections as to paragraphs 12, 16 and 17 of Choy's declaration were sustained; therefore, paragraphs 12, 16 and 17 were ordered stricken from the declaration.
The Court further heard intervenor's motion to enforce the judgment and ordered as follows:
1. Raymond Choy, in his individual capacity and as Trustee of the Choy revocable trust dated August 6, 2007, shall execute all documents submitted to him by Old Republic Title Company before March 1, 2017. If he does not comply, the Court will hold a contempt hearing.
2. The Court finds that the agreement for sale of a portion of the property by Todd Choy is null and void and the document recorded with the San Francisco Recorder Office to be expunged, details on record.
3. After close of escrow, Raymond Choy shall deliver to John Wai: the tenant security deposits, copies of leases, rental agreements, building and apartment keys, etc.
4. Mr. Ferster's attorney's fees shall be determined later. Costs shall be determined per memorandum of costs.
5. A compliance hearing is set in Department 22 of this Court, 850 Bryant Street, at 2:00 p.m. on March 1, 2017.

Order signed in open court and further read into the record by the Court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Maria L. Horcasitas of COMBS REPORTING, INC., CSR #8683 (Tel: 415-227-4060) (503/CML)
